what_it_does: Defines a ConceptV design skill entrypoint with front matter metadata, required local reference files, brand rules, product entity shapes, artifact workflows, icon usage guidance, and known design-source uncertainties.
observations: YAML front matter names the skill "conceptv-design", describes it as a ConceptV interface and asset generation aid, and marks it user-invocable.
It instructs the reader to load README.md, colors_and_type.css, logo assets, icon assets, illustrations, UI kits, and preview cards before work.
It maps ConceptV product surfaces to fixed visual shapes: User Portal circle, Property Wizard hexagon, Virtual Hubs octagon, and Vi diamond with line-fill.
It specifies typography, color roles, signature visual moves, and visual patterns to avoid.
It gives separate guidance for visual artifacts, production code requests, unguided invocations, and icon-library usage.
It includes React, static HTML, and vanilla JavaScript examples for using the icon library.
It flags substituted fonts and inferred website tone as open questions.
standouts: The file treats product entities as shape-bound visual objects.
Gold is reserved for emphasis and primary actions, while bronze carries screen titles and line illustration.
The icon guidance distinguishes sidebar emoji, diagram icons, UI chrome icons, and status dots by context.
conventions: Uses Markdown with YAML skill metadata followed by operational design instructions.
Uses imperative guidance aimed at an agent consuming a local design-system package.
Keeps brand decisions concrete through named colors, font families, asset paths, and usage contexts.
Includes explicit avoid rules alongside preferred design moves.
concerns: The file explicitly says fonts are substitutions and the actual product UI font is unknown.
The file explicitly says website tone and copy patterns were inferred because the website was offline when the system was built.
notes: This is the local instruction layer for applying ConceptV brand, entity-shape, asset, and icon conventions during interface or prototype work.
questions: Are the actual ConceptV UI font files available for tighter matching?
Is the inferred tone from product UI strings and pitch materials intended to remain in use?
Which ConceptV surface should be prioritized when an invocation gives only a broad design request?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual content: it provides instructions and references for producing ConceptV-branded interfaces, assets, prototypes, and production-facing design work.
intention_signals: It appears intended to make an agent consistently load the right local references and apply ConceptV visual rules before producing design artifacts or production UI changes.
novelty: The entity shape system ties each product surface to a specific geometric form and extends that mapping into visual reference rules.
contribution: Contributes ConceptV-specific design governance: metadata for invocation, required reference paths, product surface shape mappings, color and type constraints, asset usage rules, icon-context rules, and known uncertainty notes.
summary_for_embedding: This SKILL.md is a ConceptV design-system agent entrypoint defining brand references, product entity shapes, color/type rules, visual artifact workflows, icon-library usage, and flagged source uncertainties.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to guide an agent in producing ConceptV-branded interfaces and assets by loading local design resources and following explicit brand and UI conventions.
